title: API
description: Reach and use the you-agent-factory local HTTP API: default base URL, factory-running precondition, session-oriented flow, and the path to the operations catalog.
openingSummary: Point HTTP clients at a running local factory service—default http://localhost:7437—then follow a session-oriented read and submit flow. Exact operations live on the Reference API catalog.

howToReach.title: How To Reach
howToReach.body: The local factory service exposes an HTTP API on the operator machine. Session and API traffic use the default local base URL http://localhost:7437 unless the process that bound the listener overrides host or port. Point HTTP clients at a local Factory server you run yourself—not at a documentation host.

typicalSessionFlow.title: Typical Session Flow
typicalSessionFlow.body: Treat the live Factory Session as the unit you discover, inspect, and address. Confirm a live session on the running host before submitting work or reading status. Inspect one session by id when you need its identity and runtime projection. Submit work and read status or events against that same session id. Prefer CLI and API reads—you session list, you session show, you workflow status, and matching status or event endpoints—when you need scripted inspection. Exhaustive path, method, and schema contracts stay on the Reference API catalog.

trustBoundaries.title: Trust Boundaries
trustBoundaries.body: Treat the default local bind as an operator-accessible local surface, not a multi-tenant public product edge. Open Security / Trust Boundaries for local-bind expectations, operator versus autonomous-agent ingress paths, and sensitive artifact handling. API coverage here points to that trust documentation; it does not replace it.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start or attach a local factory so a service is listening. Point your HTTP client at http://localhost:7437 unless you overrode host or port. Confirm a live Factory Session, then call session-oriented status, event, or submit operations against that session id. Open the Reference API catalog when you need exact operations, media types, or schema components.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: API coverage here is how to interact with the local HTTP API: default base URL, factory-running precondition, trust-boundary pointer, and a typical session-oriented flow. It is not the operations catalog, not an OpenAPI playground, not a pasted OpenAPI document, and not agent CLI-ingress policy replacement—it does not render OpenAPI or an operations embed. Exact HTTP operations live on the Reference API catalog. Autonomous agents must submit work only through the CLI; dashboard submit and operator HTTP submit paths remain operator surfaces.

links.factoryRunningLabel: Start or attach a local factory before calling the API
links.sessionListLabel: Confirm a live Factory Session before submit or status reads
links.statusReadLabel: Example status read against the default local host

factoryMustBeRunning.body: A factory service must already be running before clients can call the API. Startup forms such as you or you run create or attach to that runtime; the API does not start the factory by itself. If the host is unreachable, start or attach a factory, then retry.

sessionListConfirm.body: Session list is the primary liveness check on a running host. An empty list means the host responded but no live session is registered yet. A connection failure means nothing is listening on that host and port.

sessionShowNote.body: Replace <session-id> with a SESSION ID from session list. On single-session local hosts the default compatibility session is often ~default. When more than one session exists, target the intended session id rather than assuming the default.

statusReadNote.body: GET /factory-sessions/{session_id}/status is a published deeper runtime health read for one live Factory Session. Use the same host and port as the API base URL. Exact response fields and other operations stay on the Reference API catalog and Metrics documentation.

operatorIngress.body: Autonomous agents must submit work only through the CLI. Dashboard submit and POST /factory-sessions/{session_id}/work remain operator paths—do not treat them as parallel agent control ingress. See Security / Trust Boundaries for the full local-bind and ingress policy.

operationsCatalogPointer.body: Exact path, method, media-type, and schema contracts live on the Reference API operations catalog. Follow the catalog link when you need an operation inventory.
